vision.markdown:
# Product Vision

## Project
- Name: ao-cli

## Problem
Manual orchestration of AI agents and CLI workflows is fragmented and unsafe, inconsistent, and hard to observe.

Refinement context: Manual AI-agent and CLI orchestration in software teams lacks a single deterministic control plane, causing unsafe operations, inconsistent execution outcomes, and limited end-to-end observability for planning, execution, QA, and audit.

## Target Users
- developers and automation operators using AO
- Platform/DevEx engineers standardizing internal AI-assisted delivery workflows
- SRE/operations teams requiring auditable, policy-enforced automation in CI and local environments
- Engineering managers and technical leads who need traceable execution evidence for governance and incident review

## Goals
- Provide a deterministic, local-first Rust AO CLI that unifies project planning, execution, review/QA, and audit trails under one control plane
- Increase operator confidence through explicit safety gates, reproducible run artifacts, and auditable state transitions
- Make AO fully machine-operable via structured JSON outputs for automation and policy enforcement
- Define measurable reliability targets (e.g., run reproducibility rate, failed destructive-action prevention rate, audit trace completeness)
- Prioritize phased delivery: core deterministic execution + state integrity first, then safety/policy controls, then advanced workflow ergonomics
- Establish interoperability goals for machine consumers (stable JSON schemas, backward-compatibility policy, and validation tooling)

## Constraints
- Keep the project Rust-only with no desktop-wrapper (Tauri) dependency
- Do not replace existing AO state files with non-machine-readable outputs
- Deliver command safety through confirmations for destructive operations and controlled destructive flows
- Ensure all runtime artifacts and history are traceable through `.ao/` state, run events, and task outputs
- Maintain backward compatibility for existing `.ao` state and JSON envelopes across minor releases
- Enforce deterministic behavior under partial failure and recovery paths (daemon restarts, orphan cleanup, interrupted runs)
- Bound AI-assisted behavior with explicit policy hooks and human-approval checkpoints for high-risk actions
- Require objective observability baselines (event completeness, error taxonomy coverage, and replayability) before expanding feature surface

## Value Proposition
AO provides a deterministic, inspectable, machine-friendly CLI control plane for orchestrating agent work from vision and requirements through execution and QA with auditable outcomes. AO is the trusted Rust-native control plane for AI-enabled software operations: deterministic by default, policy-aware for safety, and fully machine-consumable with auditable evidence from intent through delivery.

## Complexity
- Tier: complex
- Confidence: 0.76
- Recommended requirement range: 14-20
- Task density: high
- Rationale: Scope spans multi-surface orchestration (planning, runtime, QA, audit), strict determinism, safety-critical flows, schema stability, and AI-in-the-loop controls. Cross-cutting requirements for recoverability, observability, and policy enforcement increase coupling and validation load beyond medium complexity.

vision.value_proposition: AO provides a deterministic, inspectable, machine-friendly CLI control plane for orchestrating agent work from vision and requirements through execution and QA with auditable outcomes. AO is the trusted Rust-native control plane for AI-enabled software operations: deterministic by default, policy-aware for safety, and fully machine-consumable with auditable evidence from intent through delivery.

complexity_assessment.rationale: Scope spans multi-surface orchestration (planning, runtime, QA, audit), strict determinism, safety-critical flows, schema stability, and AI-in-the-loop controls. Cross-cutting requirements for recoverability, observability, and policy enforcement increase coupling and validation load beyond medium complexity.

REQ-012.title: Define typed API contract and client SDK for web GUI
REQ-012.description: Establish a strict front-end contract layer that maps to orchestrator-web-server endpoints with schema validation, versioning strategy, and deterministic error handling.
acceptance_criteria: Client SDK wraps all consumed endpoints with typed request/response interfaces and centralized transport configuration.
Schema-validation or structural guards protect runtime against malformed/partial payloads from server upgrades.
Error normalization maps server failures into actionable UI states with remediation hints, preserving error codes.
API version compatibility policy is documented with explicit handling for additive and breaking changes.
Contract tests validate SDK behavior against representative success, validation-failure, not-found, and unavailable responses.

REQ-018.title: Establish testing and release discipline for web GUI
REQ-018.description: Create robust quality gates and release process for the React app so changes are reliable, observable, and safe to deploy with backend evolution.
acceptance_criteria: CI pipeline runs static checks, unit tests, and integration tests for web-app critical flows against mocked and/or local backend contracts.
End-to-end smoke suite validates login/context load, requirement operations, task/workflow actions, and run telemetry rendering.
Frontend build artifacts include version metadata tied to git commit for traceability.
Release checklist defines rollback strategy, compatibility checks versus server API versions, and post-deploy verification steps.
Regression criteria are documented for every new route/action before feature completion is accepted.

REQ-009.title: Standardize destructive action safeguards
REQ-009.description: Ensure destructive CLI operations require explicit confirmations and provide deterministic dry-run/preview semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Destructive commands require explicit operator confirmation before state-changing execution.
Each destructive command provides a non-destructive preview path or clear precondition output.

REQ-021.title: Add interactive terminal UI for ao CLI
REQ-021.description:
Deliver `ao tui` as an interactive terminal command that lets operators inspect prioritized tasks and launch agent runs from one screen while routing MCP traffic through an AO-managed internal bridge.

Constraints:
- Task data in the TUI must come from ServiceHub APIs; no direct `.ao` JSON reads in the TUI flow.
- Provider execution from TUI must be MCP-locked to an internally spawned `ao mcp serve` process via `AoCliMcpBridge`; no external MCP server dependency.
- Scope is MVP: one-screen dashboard with model selection, prompt input, output panel, and prioritized task list.
- Existing CLI command behavior outside `tui` must remain unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: `ao tui` launches an interactive terminal screen and exits cleanly on `q` or `Ctrl+C` without leaving the terminal in raw mode.
Interactive mode rejects `--json` output with a clear error message.
The screen shows MCP bridge session context and a selectable model list, and supports model navigation with `j/k` or arrow keys.
The TUI loads prioritized tasks via `ServiceHub::tasks().list_prioritized` and renders each row as `TASK-ID [status] title`.
Prompt editing and control keys are implemented: typing, `Backspace`, `Esc`, `Enter`, `r` refresh, `p` print-mode toggle, and `Ctrl+L` output clear.
Agent runs launched from TUI are MCP-locked through an internally spawned `ao mcp serve` process bridged by `AoCliMcpBridge`, with success/failure summary surfaced in status/output.
Headless mode (`ao tui --headless --prompt "<text>"`) executes one run through the same MCP lock path and returns a non-zero exit on failed runs.
CLI wiring includes the `tui` subcommand in dispatch and preserves behavior of existing non-TUI commands (validated by `cargo check` and CLI smoke tests).

REQ-008.title: Guarantee JSON schema compatibility for CLI envelopes
REQ-008.description: Define and test backward-compatible JSON envelope behavior for success/error outputs and key planning/runtime payloads.
acceptance_criteria: Schema validation tests cover ao.cli.v1 success and error envelopes for key command groups.
Breaking schema changes require explicit version bump and migration note.

REQ-001.title: Define and maintain the ao-cli self-hosting operating workflow
REQ-001.description:
Contributors can reliably use `ao` to plan, execute, and close work in this repository without undocumented steps.

**Implementation boundary:** This requirement covers contributor-facing workflow documentation and repository operating rules (`README` and `AGENTS.md`) for this repo only. It does not include CLI feature development.
REQ-001.body:
Contributors can reliably use `ao` to plan, execute, and close work in this repository without undocumented steps.

**Implementation boundary:** This requirement covers contributor-facing workflow documentation and repository operating rules (`README` and `AGENTS.md`) for this repo only. It does not include CLI feature development.
acceptance_criteria: README includes a self-hosting workflow section with at least one complete command sequence from requirement selection through task/requirement closure.
All documented commands in the self-hosting section include explicit `--project-root "$(pwd)"` usage where automation is shown.
AGENTS.md documents CLI-only `.ao` mutation policy and task logging expectations (before work, during work, completion).
README and AGENTS.md contain no conflicting instructions for project root resolution, daemon registry usage, or status transitions.
A new contributor can execute the documented workflow on a clean clone and produce at least one updated task status without manual `.ao` file edits.

REQ-015.title: Provide real-time run telemetry and artifact inspection UX
REQ-015.description: Expose live run execution insight including event streams, JSONL outputs, artifacts, and error surfaces so operators can diagnose and recover quickly.
acceptance_criteria: Run detail view streams and renders events incrementally with stable ordering and clear status derivation (running/completed/failed).
JSONL viewer supports source-file filtering, keyword search, and timestamp navigation for large logs.
Artifact browser lists files with metadata and supports safe download/read actions through backend endpoints.
Error panels map runtime failures to suggested operator next steps (retry, inspect runner health, open history/error details).
UI supports degraded mode when streaming is unavailable, falling back to periodic polling with explicit indicator.

REQ-019.title: Add first-class web UI observability and debug logging
REQ-019.description: Instrument the React web GUI with structured client telemetry, correlation IDs, and operator-facing diagnostics so failures can be triaged quickly against backend run/events.
acceptance_criteria: Client logs are structured and include correlation context (route, action, request id/run id/task id when available).
Error events are captured at boundary, query, and mutation layers with severity and remediation metadata.
Operators can access a diagnostics panel that surfaces recent failed actions and linked backend artifacts/events.
Logging supports redaction rules to avoid exposing secrets or sensitive payloads in UI-visible traces.
Telemetry export format is documented and can be consumed by downstream monitoring tools.

REQ-014.title: Deliver operational task and workflow control center
REQ-014.description: Create a unified interface for task backlog management, workflow execution controls, phase status visibility, and gated approvals.
acceptance_criteria: Task board supports prioritized queue, status transitions, assignment, dependency visualization, and checklist interactions via API-backed mutations.
Workflow views expose run/resume/pause/cancel operations with explicit confirmation and post-action telemetry feedback.
Phase timeline presents current phase, checkpoint state, decisions, approvals, and blocking reasons with timestamped history.
UI enforces rule-based action availability (disabled states and rationale) based on workflow/task status and server responses.
Operator actions are idempotent on retry and display conflict-resolution messaging when backend state has changed.

REQ-013.title: Implement end-to-end planning UX for vision and requirements
REQ-013.description: Provide complete interactive flows for drafting/refining vision, drafting/refining requirements, and reviewing requirement quality and links in one coherent workspace.
acceptance_criteria: Vision editor supports structured sections (problem, users, goals, constraints, value proposition, complexity) with live preview and diff visibility.
Requirements workspace supports list/detail split view, filter/sort/search, and inline lifecycle transitions with audit-safe confirmation flows.
Requirement detail page visualizes acceptance criteria, status, linked tasks, and traceability context without raw JSON exposure by default.
Refine/draft actions expose progress, model/tool metadata, fallback behavior, and deterministic completion states.
All planning actions preserve deep links and refresh-safe state so operators can resume after navigation or reload.

REQ-005.title: Establish Rust-only CI quality gates for workspace health
REQ-005.description:
Changes are validated consistently so regressions are caught before merge across AO’s core Rust workspace.

**Implementation boundary:** CI checks for compile/test/smoke quality gates in this repository’s Rust crates. Excludes release packaging/distribution mechanics.
REQ-005.body:
Changes are validated consistently so regressions are caught before merge across AO’s core Rust workspace.

**Implementation boundary:** CI checks for compile/test/smoke quality gates in this repository’s Rust crates. Excludes release packaging/distribution mechanics.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs `cargo check` for `orchestrator-cli`, `orchestrator-core`, `agent-runner`, `llm-cli-wrapper`, and `llm-mcp-server` on every pull request.
CI includes smoke checks for `ao --help` and `llm-cli-wrapper --help` with non-zero failure on command regressions.
CI configuration explicitly avoids desktop-wrapper/Tauri dependencies and fails if prohibited dependencies are introduced.
At least one CI job executes targeted automated tests for runtime or integration surfaces touched by scoped crates.
CI results are visible as required status checks for merge in the default branch workflow.

REQ-007.title: Enforce Rust-only dependency guardrails
REQ-007.description: Prevent desktop-wrapper drift by enforcing Rust-only workspace dependencies and validating prohibited frameworks are excluded.
acceptance_criteria: CI fails when prohibited desktop-wrapper dependencies are introduced in workspace crates.
Dependency policy documentation lists allowed and prohibited dependency classes.

REQ-002.title: Improve daily CLI ergonomics for core ao command groups
REQ-002.description:
Developers and operators can discover and run core commands faster with consistent help text and actionable input-error guidance.

**Implementation boundary:** Applies to `orchestrator-cli` command UX for `project`, `requirements`, `task`, `workflow`, and `execute`. Excludes runtime reliability concerns and integration behavior of external binaries.
REQ-002.body:
Developers and operators can discover and run core commands faster with consistent help text and actionable input-error guidance.

**Implementation boundary:** Applies to `orchestrator-cli` command UX for `project`, `requirements`, `task`, `workflow`, and `execute`. Excludes runtime reliability concerns and integration behavior of external binaries.
acceptance_criteria: Each core command group has a non-empty top-level help description and subcommand list with consistent structure and terminology.
At least 10 representative invalid-input scenarios return actionable errors that include both the failure reason and a concrete correction hint.
All `--json` error responses for invalid input conform to the `ao.cli.v1` envelope with `ok=false`, `error.code`, and `exit_code=2`.
Help output examples and command names in documentation match actual CLI behavior for the five scoped command groups.
Regression tests verify help output snapshots and error-shape consistency for the scoped groups.

REQ-017.title: Meet accessibility, responsiveness, and performance baselines
REQ-017.description: Ship the web GUI with enterprise-grade usability quality across desktop and mobile form factors, keyboard users, and assistive technologies.
acceptance_criteria: Core flows (planning, tasks, workflows, run inspection) are fully keyboard navigable with visible focus states.
ARIA labels, semantic landmarks, and form validation messaging meet WCAG-oriented accessibility expectations for primary interactions.
Responsive breakpoints preserve full functionality on common laptop and mobile viewport classes without hidden critical controls.
Perceived performance budget is defined and met for initial load and key route transitions under realistic project data sizes.
Empty/loading/error states are intentionally designed and consistent across all route families.

REQ-016.title: Implement safety rails for high-risk operations in web UI
REQ-016.description: Ensure destructive or irreversible actions in the web app are guarded by explicit operator intent confirmation, previews, and auditability.
acceptance_criteria: Destructive actions require typed confirmation or equivalent high-friction acknowledgement before request submission.
Where supported by backend, UI presents dry-run/preview output before executing destructive mutations.
Every high-risk action writes auditable context into UI-visible activity stream (actor, timestamp, target, outcome).
Permission and state preconditions are validated in UI and revalidated against server response before final success messaging.
Bulk operations include partial-failure reporting with deterministic rollback/compensation guidance.

REQ-010.title: Improve run/event traceability completeness
REQ-010.description: Ensure every workflow and agent run emits consistent trace artifacts that can be replayed for audit and debugging.
acceptance_criteria: Run artifacts include timestamps, identifiers, and event sequencing metadata sufficient for replay.
Output commands can locate run artifacts from scoped runtime storage without manual path discovery.

REQ-011.title: Build React web-app shell over orchestrator-web-server
REQ-011.description: Create a production-grade React app that serves as the primary operator console for AO, wrapping orchestrator-web-server capabilities with clear IA, deterministic state handling, and safe command execution pathways.
acceptance_criteria: Web app bootstraps with route-level code splitting and typed API integration for all core AO domains (vision, requirements, tasks, workflow, runs, errors, history).
Navigation model includes global project context, queue dashboards, and deep-linkable pages for each entity ID.
All UI mutations execute through server APIs only; no direct file assumptions or local state hacks bypassing backend contracts.
Global error boundaries and retry affordances are implemented for route, data-fetch, and action failures.
State management strategy is documented and enforced (query caching, optimistic updates policy, invalidation rules).

REQ-003.title: Harden agent-runner lifecycle reliability and diagnostics
REQ-003.description:
Operator confidence increases because runner start, health, and shutdown behavior is predictable and failures are diagnosable.

**Implementation boundary:** Covers `agent-runner` process lifecycle, health signaling, and diagnostics emitted to AO consumers. Excludes wrapper/MCP endpoint orchestration.
REQ-003.body:
Operator confidence increases because runner start, health, and shutdown behavior is predictable and failures are diagnosable.

**Implementation boundary:** Covers `agent-runner` process lifecycle, health signaling, and diagnostics emitted to AO consumers. Excludes wrapper/MCP endpoint orchestration.
acceptance_criteria: Automated tests cover normal startup, graceful shutdown, forced termination handling, and restart recovery scenarios.
Health-check failures emit structured diagnostics including failure category, timestamp, and actionable context.
Runner lifecycle events are observable through existing AO event/output pathways without requiring ad-hoc log parsing.
Repeated start/stop cycling (minimum 20 cycles in test) completes without orphaned runner processes.
Documented recovery steps exist for at least three failure modes: socket bind failure, dependency unavailability, and stale process state.

REQ-004.title: Standardize llm-cli-wrapper and llm-mcp-server integration contract
REQ-004.description:
Integration between wrapper and MCP server becomes predictable, reducing setup drift across environments.

**Implementation boundary:** Defines build/startup/endpoint-resolution contract and smoke validation between `llm-cli-wrapper` and `llm-mcp-server`. Excludes agent-runner lifecycle and general CLI help UX.
REQ-004.body:
Integration between wrapper and MCP server becomes predictable, reducing setup drift across environments.

**Implementation boundary:** Defines build/startup/endpoint-resolution contract and smoke validation between `llm-cli-wrapper` and `llm-mcp-server`. Excludes agent-runner lifecycle and general CLI help UX.
acceptance_criteria: A documented integration contract specifies required startup inputs, endpoint discovery behavior, and expected health semantics.
A reproducible smoke test validates server startup plus at least one health and one agent-related endpoint interaction.
Wrapper behavior on MCP server unavailability returns deterministic, structured error output with clear remediation hint.
Build and startup commands used by the integration path are documented and executable from repository root.
Contract tests run in CI and fail on breaking changes to wrapper-to-server startup or endpoint resolution behavior.

REQ-006.title: Define reproducible multi-binary release packaging process
REQ-006.description:
Operators can obtain consistent release artifacts for AO binaries with clear naming, platform scope, and verification steps.

**Implementation boundary:** Covers packaging workflow and artifact conventions for `ao`, `agent-runner`, `llm-cli-wrapper`, and `llm-mcp-server`. Excludes CI PR quality gates and runtime behavior guarantees.
REQ-006.body:
Operators can obtain consistent release artifacts for AO binaries with clear naming, platform scope, and verification steps.

**Implementation boundary:** Covers packaging workflow and artifact conventions for `ao`, `agent-runner`, `llm-cli-wrapper`, and `llm-mcp-server`. Excludes CI PR quality gates and runtime behavior guarantees.
acceptance_criteria: Release documentation defines artifact naming convention, supported target platforms, and checksum/verification procedure.
An automated release build path produces all four required binaries in one run.
Each produced artifact includes version metadata traceable to a git commit or tag.
Release outputs are organized in a deterministic directory structure suitable for scripted consumption.
A dry-run release can be executed locally or in CI to validate packaging without publishing.

REQ-020.title: Architecture knowledge graph support
REQ-020.description: Add architecture entities/edges JSON graph and task linkage so tasks can indicate where changes should live
